title: Authors and Citation

code: @Manual{,   title = {alkahest: Pre-Processing XY Data from Experimental Methods},   author = {Nicolas Frerebeau},   year = {2022},   note = {https://packages.tesselle.org/alkahest/, https://github.com/tesselle/alkahest}, }

title: Pre-Processing XY Data from Experimental Methods
text: Pre-Processing XY Data Experimental Methods. alkahest toolbox pre-processing XY data experimental methods (.e. signal can measured along continuous variable). provides methods baseline correction, smoothing, normalization integration. package depends base R packages.

title: Pre-Processing XY Data from Experimental Methods
text: can install released version alkahest CRAN : development version GitHub :

title: Pre-Processing XY Data from Experimental Methods
text: alkahest expects input data simplest form (two-column matrix data frame, two-element list two numeric vectors). exceptions, functions return list two elements x y.
code: ## Load the package library(alkahest) ## gamma-ray spectrometry data("BEGe")  ## Subset from 3 to 200 keV BEGe <- signal_select(BEGe, from = 3, to = 200)  ## SNIP baseline BEGe_snip <- baseline_snip(BEGe, LLS = FALSE, decreasing = FALSE, n = 100)  plot(BEGe, type = "l", xlab = "Energy (keV)", ylab = "Count") lines(BEGe_snip, type = "l", col = "red") ## gamma-ray spectrometry data("LaBr")  ## Subset from 1350 to 1650 keV LaBr <- signal_select(LaBr, from = 1350, to = 1650)  ## Plot raw spectrum plot(LaBr, type = "l", xlab = "Energy (keV)", ylab = "Count", main = "Raw data")  ## Rectangular smoothing LaBr_unweighted <- smooth_rectangular(LaBr, m = 3) plot(LaBr_unweighted, type = "l", xlab = "Energy (keV)", ylab = "Count",      main = "Rectangular smoothing")   ## Triangular smoothing LaBr_weighted <- smooth_triangular(LaBr, m = 5) plot(LaBr_weighted, type = "l", xlab = "Energy (keV)", ylab = "Count",      main = "Triangular smoothing")  ## Savitzky–Golay filter LaBr_savitzky <- smooth_savitzky(LaBr, m = 21, p = 2) plot(LaBr_savitzky, type = "l", xlab = "Energy (keV)", ylab = "Count",      main = "Savitzky–Golay filter")

title: Pre-Processing XY Data from Experimental Methods

title: Baseline Estimation — baseline
text: Baseline Estimation

title: Baseline Estimation — baseline
code: baseline_linear(x, y, ...)  baseline_rubberband(x, y, ...)  baseline_snip(x, y, ...)  # S4 method for numeric,numeric baseline_linear(x, y, from = min(x), to = max(x))  # S4 method for ANY,missing baseline_linear(x, from = min(x), to = max(x))  # S4 method for numeric,numeric baseline_rubberband(x, y, noise = 0, spline = TRUE, ...)  # S4 method for ANY,missing baseline_rubberband(x, noise = 0, spline = TRUE, ...)  # S4 method for numeric,numeric baseline_snip(x, y, LLS = FALSE, decreasing = FALSE, n = 100, ...)  # S4 method for ANY,missing baseline_snip(x, LLS = FALSE, decreasing = FALSE, n = 100, ...)

title: Baseline Estimation — baseline
text: x, y numeric vector. y missing, attempt made interpret x suitable way (see grDevices::xy.coords()). ... Extra parameters passed methods. numeric value giving first data point (x unit) used linear interpolation. integer value giving last data point (x unit) used linear interpolation. noise length-one numeric vector giving noise level. used method "rubberband". spline logical scalar: spline interpolation support points used instead linear interpolation? used method "rubberband". LLS logical scalar: LLS operator applied x employing SNIP algorithm? used method "SNIP". decreasing logical scalar: decreasing clipping window used? used method "SNIP". n integer value giving number iterations. used method "SNIP".

title: Baseline Estimation — baseline
text: Returns list two components x y.

title: Baseline Estimation — baseline
text: following methods available baseline estimation: SNIP Sensitive Nonlinear Iterative Peak clipping algorithm. rubberband convex envelope spectrum determined baseline estimated part convex envelope lying spectrum. Note rubber band enter concave regions () spectrum. linear Linear baseline estimation.

title: Baseline Estimation — baseline
text: baseline_rubberband() slightly modified C. Beleites' hyperSpec::spc.rubberband().

title: Baseline Estimation — baseline
text: Liland, K. H. (2015). 4S Peak Filling - baseline estimation iterative mean suppression. MethodsX, 2, 135-140. doi:10.1016/j.mex.2015.02.009 . Morháč, M., Kliman, J., Matoušek, V., Veselský, M. & Turzo, . (1997). Background elimination methods multidimensional gamma-ray spectra. Nuclear Instruments Methods Physics Research Section : Accelerators, Spectrometers, Detectors Associated Equipment, 401(1), p. 113-132. doi:10.1016/S0168-9002(97)01023-1 Morháč, M. & Matoušek, V. (2008). Peak Clipping Algorithms Background Estimation Spectroscopic Data. Applied Spectroscopy, 62(1), p. 91-106. doi:10.1366/000370208783412762 Ryan, C. G., Clayton, E., Griffin, W. L., Sie, S. H. & Cousens, D. R. (1988). SNIP, statistics-sensitive background treatment quantitative analysis PIXE spectra geoscience applications. Nuclear Instruments Methods Physics Research Section B: Beam Interactions Materials Atoms, 34(3), p. 396-402. doi:10.1016/0168-583X(88)90063-8

title: Baseline Estimation — baseline
text: N. Frerebeau

title: Baseline Estimation — baseline
code: ## gamma-ray spectrometry data("BEGe")  ## Subset from 2.75 to 200 keV BEGe <- signal_select(BEGe, from = 3, to = 200)  ## Plot spectrum plot(BEGe, type = "l", xlab = "Energy (keV)", ylab = "Count")  ## Linear baseline BEGe_linear <- baseline_linear(x = BEGe, from = 8, to = 20)  plot(BEGe, type = "l", xlab = "Energy (keV)", ylab = "Count") lines(BEGe_linear, type = "l", col = "red")   ## Rubberband baseline BEGe_rubber <- baseline_rubberband(BEGe)  plot(BEGe, type = "l", xlab = "Energy (keV)", ylab = "Count") lines(BEGe_rubber, type = "l", col = "red")   ## SNIP baseline BEGe_snip <- baseline_snip(BEGe, LLS = FALSE, decreasing = FALSE, n = 100)  plot(BEGe, type = "l", xlab = "Energy (keV)", ylab = "Count") lines(BEGe_snip, type = "l", col = "red")

title: Normalize Intensities — rescale
text: rescale_total() rescales intensities sum specified value. rescale_range(), rescale_min() andrescale_max() rescales intensities specified minimum maximum.

title: Normalize Intensities — rescale
code: rescale_total(x, y, ...)  rescale_range(x, y, ...)  rescale_min(x, y, ...)  rescale_max(x, y, ...)  # S4 method for numeric,numeric rescale_total(x, y, total = 1)  # S4 method for ANY,missing rescale_total(x, total = 1)  # S4 method for numeric,numeric rescale_range(x, y, min = 0, max = 1)  # S4 method for ANY,missing rescale_range(x, min = 0, max = 1)  # S4 method for numeric,numeric rescale_min(x, y, min = 0)  # S4 method for ANY,missing rescale_min(x, min = 0)  # S4 method for numeric,numeric rescale_max(x, y, max = 1)  # S4 method for ANY,missing rescale_max(x, max = 1)

title: Normalize Intensities — rescale
text: x, y numeric vector. y missing, attempt made interpret x suitable way (see grDevices::xy.coords()). ... Currently used. total legnth-one numeric vector specifying output total. min legnth-one numeric vector specifying output minimum. max legnth-one numeric vector specifying output maximum.

title: Normalize Intensities — rescale
text: Returns list two components x y.

title: Normalize Intensities — rescale
text: N. Frerebeau

title: Normalize Intensities — rescale
code: ## gamma-ray spectrometry data("BEGe")  ## Subset from 2.75 to 200 keV BEGe <- signal_select(BEGe, from = 3, to = 200)  ## Plot spectrum plot(BEGe, type = "l", xlab = "Energy (keV)", ylab = "Count")   ## Rescale so that intensities sum to 1 BEGe_total <- rescale_total(BEGe, total = 1) plot(BEGe_total, type = "l", xlab = "Energy (keV)", ylab = "Count")   ## Rescale intensities to 0-1 BEGe_range <- rescale_range(BEGe, min = 0, max = 1) plot(BEGe_range, type = "l", xlab = "Energy (keV)", ylab = "Count")

title: Smooth — smooth
text: Smoothes intensities.

title: Smooth — smooth
code: smooth_rectangular(x, y, ...)  smooth_triangular(x, y, ...)  smooth_loess(x, y, ...)  smooth_savitzky(x, y, ...)  # S4 method for numeric,numeric smooth_rectangular(x, y, m = 3)  # S4 method for ANY,missing smooth_rectangular(x, m)  # S4 method for numeric,numeric smooth_triangular(x, y, m = 3)  # S4 method for ANY,missing smooth_triangular(x, m)  # S4 method for numeric,numeric smooth_loess(x, y, span = 0.75)  # S4 method for ANY,missing smooth_loess(x, span = 0.75)  # S4 method for numeric,numeric smooth_savitzky(x, y, m = 3, p = 2)  # S4 method for ANY,missing smooth_savitzky(x, m, p)

title: Smooth — smooth
text: x, y numeric vector. y missing, attempt made interpret x suitable way (see grDevices::xy.coords()). ... Currently used. m odd integer giving number adjacent points used. span integer specifying degree smoothing (see stats::loess()). p integer giving degree polynomial used.

title: Smooth — smooth
text: Returns list two components x y.

title: Smooth — smooth
text: following smoothing methods available: rectangular Unweighted sliding-average rectangular smooth. replaces point signal average \(m\) adjacent points. triangular Weighted sliding-average triangular smooth. replaces point signal weighted mean \(m\) adjacent points. loess Smooth Loess fitting. savitzky Savitzky-Golay filter. method based least-squares fitting polynomials segments \(m\) adjacent points. \((m - 1) / 2\) points beginning end spectrum complete \(m\)-width smooth calculated. prevent data loss, progressively smaller smooths used ends spectrum smooth_rectangular() smooth_triangular(). Savitzky-Golay filter used, original \((m - 1) / 2\) points ends spectrum preserved.

title: Smooth — smooth
text: Gorry, P. . (1990). General Least-Squares Smoothing Differentiation Convolution (Savitzky-Golay) Method. Analytical Chemistry, 62(6), p. 570-573. doi:10.1021/ac00205a007 . Savitzky, . & Golay, M. J. E. (1964). Smoothing Differentiation Data Simplified Least Squares Procedures. Analytical Chemistry, 36(8), p. 1627-1639. doi:10.1021/ac60214a047 .

title: Smooth — smooth
text: N. Frerebeau

title: Smooth — smooth
code: ## gamma-ray spectrometry data("LaBr")  ## Plot spectrum LaBr <- signal_select(LaBr, from = 1350, to = 1650) plot(LaBr, type = "l", xlab = "Energy (keV)", ylab = "Count")   ## Rectangular smoothing LaBr_unweighted <- smooth_rectangular(LaBr, m = 3) plot(LaBr_unweighted, type = "l", xlab = "Energy (keV)", ylab = "Count")   ## Triangular smoothing LaBr_weighted <- smooth_triangular(LaBr, m = 5) plot(LaBr_weighted, type = "l", xlab = "Energy (keV)", ylab = "Count")   ## Loess smoothing LaBr_loess <- smooth_loess(LaBr, span = 0.75) plot(LaBr_loess, type = "l", xlab = "Energy (keV)", ylab = "Count")   ## Savitzky–Golay filter LaBr_savitzky <- smooth_savitzky(LaBr, m = 21, p = 2) plot(LaBr_savitzky, type = "l", xlab = "Energy (keV)", ylab = "Count")

title: Subset — subset
text: signal_select() allows subset values x. signal_slice() allows subset position along x.

title: Subset — subset
code: signal_select(x, y, ...)  signal_slice(x, y, ...)  # S4 method for numeric,numeric signal_select(x, y, from, to)  # S4 method for ANY,missing signal_select(x, from, to)  # S4 method for numeric,numeric signal_slice(x, y, subset)  # S4 method for ANY,missing signal_slice(x, subset)

title: Subset — subset
text: x, y numeric vector. y missing, attempt made interpret x suitable way (see grDevices::xy.coords()). ... Currently used. , numeric value giving first last value (x unit) selected. subset integer vector giving either positive values keep, negative values drop. values provided must either positive negative (coerced integer .integer()).

title: Subset — subset
text: Returns list two components x y.

title: Subset — subset
text: N. Frerebeau

title: Subset — subset
code: ## gamma-ray spectrometry data("BEGe")  ## Plot spectrum plot(BEGe, type = "l", xlab = "Energy (keV)", ylab = "Count")   ## Subset from 2.75 keV to 200 keV BEGe_1 <- signal_select(BEGe, from = 3, to = 200)  ## Plot spectrum plot(BEGe_1, type = "l", xlab = "Energy (keV)", ylab = "Count")   ## Subset from the 20th to the 1250th value BEGe_2 <- signal_slice(BEGe, subset = 20:1250)  ## Plot spectrum plot(BEGe_2, type = "l", xlab = "Energy (keV)", ylab = "Count")

title: Transform Intensities — signal_transform
text: Transform Intensities

title: Transform Intensities — signal_transform
code: signal_transform(x, y, ...)  # S4 method for numeric,numeric signal_transform(x, y, f, ...)  # S4 method for ANY,missing signal_transform(x, f, ...)

title: Transform Intensities — signal_transform
text: x, y numeric vector. y missing, attempt made interpret x suitable way (see grDevices::xy.coords()). ... Extra arguments passed f. f function takes numeric vector intensities argument returns numeric vector.

title: Transform Intensities — signal_transform
text: Returns list two components x y.

title: Transform Intensities — signal_transform
text: stabilization step aims improving identification peaks low signal--noise ratio. particularly targets higher energy peaks.

title: Transform Intensities — signal_transform
text: N. Frerebeau

title: Transform Intensities — signal_transform
code: ## gamma-ray spectrometry data("BEGe")  ## Subset from 2.75 to 200 keV BEGe <- signal_select(BEGe, from = 3, to = 200)  ## Plot spectrum plot(BEGe, type = "l", xlab = "Energy (keV)", ylab = "Count")   ## Transform intensities BEGe_trans <- signal_transform(BEGe, f = sqrt) plot(BEGe_trans, type = "l", xlab = "Energy (keV)", ylab = "sqrt(Count)")
